title: mRemoteNG prevent using AltGr keys
author: LTHxxx
body_snippet:
mRemoteNG prevent using AltGr keys

## Expected Behavior
mRemoteNG should not prevent using AltGr keys

## Current Behavior
double screen configuration with one editor (notepad++, notepad, ultraedit) on main screen and mRemoteNG expanded on second screen, from times to times AltGr keys are not available on the keyboard, iconizing MRemoteNG fix the trouble

## Possible Solution


## Steps to Reproduce (for bugs)
when it occurs, reducing the mRemoteNG windows fix it...

## Context
k...

iterations.description: AltGr (sent as Ctrl+Alt on Windows) is likely being intercepted by mRemoteNG's keyboard hook before reaching the remote session, breaking input for European keyboard layouts.

comments.author: mheden
comments.snippet:
I see the same issue. Sometime when having mRemoteNG open the AltGr stop working in my host os. When I'm closing mRemoteNG the button starts working again.

Also Win10 and double screen configuration (though I'm not sure the double screen matter).

comments.author: simonai1254
comments.snippet:
That's not a mRemoteNG but a RDP thing. Just restart all RDP sessions and you are good to go. mRemoteNG can't do anything about it.

And in case you need to use AltGr and can't restart everything first, use Ctrl+Alt instead.

comments.author: LIyoai
comments.snippet: Sometimes it‘s also enough just to click in a rdp session and AltGr works again on the host.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Our fork ...

notes:
AI triage (claude): AltGr (sent as Ctrl+Alt on Windows) is likely being intercepted by mRemoteNG's keyboard hook before reaching the remote session, breaking input for European keyboard layouts.
Approach: Detect AltGr (Right Alt / Ctrl+Alt combination via e.KeyData or WM_KEYDOWN with extended key flag) in the key interception layer and allow it to pass through without consuming the synthetic Ctrl press; mirror the dead-key fix applied in #1742 for VNC and the scan-code fix in #1728 for RDP.
